parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff fully deletes scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) and the commit log explicitly admits a resulting ReferenceError ('Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge'). Four successive hotfix bundles (C, D3+, E+, F) were required to stabilize after deletions, which is a strong signal the cleanup was iterative and potentially incomplete. Without verifying zero remaining import references to the deleted module, a latent runtime crash path may exist. No other findings are defensible from the metadata alone, but this single risk is sufficient to warrant confirmation before shipping.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs may leave dangling imports causing runtime ReferenceError
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was fully removed in this diff range. The commit 'fix: Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge' explicitly acknowledges a ReferenceError resulted from earlier deletions. The iterative pattern of 4+ hotfix bundles (C, D3+, E+, F), each fixing regressions from the prior round, indicates the cleanup was not clean on first or second attempt. check-imports.mjs was modified (5 lines) but its scope is unknown from the diff stat alone. Any remaining `import` of process.mjs in glm-companion.mjs (139 lines changed), glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed), job-control.mjs (20 lines changed), tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed), or other modules will cause an unconditional synchronous crash at import time — a failure that is silent until the affected code path is first exercised at runtime.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs\|from.*["\x27].*/process["\x27]' scripts/` against the v0.4.6 tree to confirm zero remaining references. If any exist, this is a ship-blocker. Additionally, add a CI gate that statically verifies all import targets resolve to existing files — this is broader than check-imports.mjs which may only validate specific patterns.

next_steps: Grep the v0.4.6 tree for any remaining import/require referencing process.mjs under scripts/
Run the full test suite (node --test tests/*.test.mjs) against the v0.4.6 tree and confirm zero failures
If dangling imports are found, do not ship — the runtime crash is unconditional on the affected path
If no dangling imports are found, document the verification in the release notes and close this finding
